back_translation: Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:
'Should such a multitude of words go unanswered, and should a talkative man be vindicated?
Should your babbling silence others? Will you mock without anyone rebuking you?
You have said, 'My teaching is pure, and I am clean in Your sight.'
But if only God would speak and open His lips against you,
and reveal to you the secrets of wisdom—for true wisdom has two sides—know then that God exacts from you less than your iniquity deserves.
Can you fathom the deep things of God? Can you find the limits of the Almighty?
They are higher than the heavens—what can you do? Deeper than Sheol—what can you know?
Their measure is longer than the earth and wider than the sea.
If He passes by and imprisons, and convenes a court, who can stop Him?
For He knows deceitful men; when He sees iniquity, will He not take note?
But a foolish man will gain understanding only when the colt of a wild donkey is born a man.
As for you, if you set your heart aright and stretch out your hands to Him,
if you put away the iniquity in your hand and let no injustice dwell in your tents,
then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will stand firm and unafraid.
For you will forget your misery, remembering it like waters that have passed by.
Your life will be brighter than noonday, and its darkness will be like the morning.
You will be secure, for there is hope; you will look around and lie down in safety.
You will lie down with no one to frighten you, and many will court your favor.
But the eyes of the wicked will fail; their way of escape will be lost, and their hope will be but a last breath.'
